TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan ka mangtjengtjez qemiladj i Siniɫizaw a salasaladj ni Salakats: ti Qaquangan ti Paɫaiɫai ti Tequng ti Qavulung ti Vaɫuan ti Taupili.
ka sicuayan ka mangtjengtjez qemiladj i Siniljizaw a salasaladj ni Salakac: ti Qaquangan ti Paljailjai ti Tequng ti Qavulung ti Valjuan ti Taupili.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time the companions of Salakats came together and sat down at Siniɫizaw: (the family heads of) Qaquangan, Paɫaiɫai, Tequng, Qavulung, Vaɫuan and Taupili.

S.FORM: "kitjen a imazanga i Siniɫizaw," aya a varung niamadju.
"kitjen a imazanga i Siniljizaw," aya a varung niamadju.
S.TRANSL: “We’ll stay here at Siniɫizaw,” they thought.

S.FORM: manu izua vatu vuqavuqaɫ.
manu izua vatu vuqavuqalj.
S.TRANSL: But there was a white dog.

S.FORM: qau qemengal azua vatu; inika djuɫe i Siniɫizaw.
qau qemengal azua vatu; inika djulje i Siniljizaw.
S.TRANSL: This dog howled and wouldn’t settle down at Siniɫizaw.

S.FORM: "tjamanguaq a tji navaik, aya a varung nua vatu.
"tjamanguaq a tji navaik, aya a varung nua vatu.
S.TRANSL: “The dog thinks we would do better to leave here.

S.FORM: masa inika djuɫe a vatu," aya.
masa inika djulje a vatu," aya.
S.TRANSL: It seems the dog won’t settle down,” they said.

S.FORM: qau mirava tua nanemanemanga mapuɫat; sa vaik.
qau mirava tua nanemanemanga mapuljat; sa vaik.
S.TRANSL: So they all got everything ready, and left.

S.FORM: semekez i Tjanumaq a vatu.
semekez i Tjanumaq a vatu.
S.TRANSL: The dog rested at Tjanumaq.

S.FORM: "masa uri a imaza itjen, a semekez a vatu," aya.
"masa uri a imaza itjen, a semekez a vatu," aya.
S.TRANSL: “It seems we’ll stay here; the dog is resting,” they said.

S.FORM: qau vaik a uta a vatu.
qau vaik a uta a vatu.
S.TRANSL: But the dog went on again,

S.FORM: qau semekez itjua Ḍaqaḍaqa.
qau semekez itjua Ḍaqadraqa.
S.TRANSL: and then rested at Ḍaqaḍaqa.

S.FORM: qau semekez a tsautsau.
qau semekez a caucau.
S.TRANSL: The people rested too.

S.FORM: "masa uri a imaza itjen," aya.
"masa uri a imaza itjen," aya.
S.TRANSL: “It seems we’ll stay here,” they said.

S.FORM: qau vaik a uta a vatu, tjaɫu zua i tja tapaw tutsu.
qau vaik a uta a vatu, tjalju zua i tja tapaw tucu.
S.TRANSL: But the dog went on again, and reached where our houses are now.

S.FORM: kemali tua qipu a vatu sa qazaw.
kemali tua qipu a vatu sa qazaw.
S.TRANSL: The dog dug in the ground and lay down.

S.FORM: "masa uri a imazanga a kadjuɫean nua vatu, a qemazaw imaza.
"masa uri a imazanga a kadjuljean nua vatu, a qemazaw imaza.
S.TRANSL: “It seems that this will be where the dog will settle down at last, as it’s lying down here.

S.FORM: tja manguaq a tji naseman tapaw," aya maɫavar.
tja manguaq a tji naseman tapaw," aya maljavar.
S.TRANSL: We would do better to build houses,” they said to one another.

S.FORM: qau seman tapaw a mapuɫat.
qau seman tapaw a mapuljat.
S.TRANSL: So they all built houses.

S.FORM: sa azazua vatu ini anga ka garavagaw.
sa azazua vatu ini anga ka garavagaw.
S.TRANSL: And that dog didn’t move any more.

S.FORM: saka tsuay anga masan talau anga, aya a tjautsikel a masi
saka cuay anga masan talau anga, aya a tjaucikel a masi
S.TRANSL: tsuay.
